posts.title: Thread: How we built an AI sales agent that books meetings on autopilot
posts.body:
We built an AI agent that handles the entire top-of-funnel sales process.

From finding leads on X to booking calls -- fully autonomous.

Here's exactly how it works (thread):

1/ Lead Discovery
Our agent monitors X 24/7 for buying signals:
- Funding announcements
- Hiring posts (especially sales roles)
- Pain-point discussions about outbound

It processes ~100 signals/day and filters down to the top 20.

2/ Research & Enrichment
For each lead, our Research Agent pulls:
- Funding history & amount
- Team size & growth rate
- Tech stack & product focus
- Recent news & social activity

Avg enrichment time: 45 seconds per lead.

3/ Lead Scoring
A scoring model ranks each lead 0-100 based on:
- ICP fit
- Timing signals
- Engagement level
- Budget indicators

Only 65+ scores get outreach. This keeps our response rate above 14%.

4/ Personalized Outreach
The agent crafts unique messages referencing:
- Their specific funding round
- Recent tweets or posts
- Relevant pain points for their stage

No templates. Every message is 1-of-1.

5/ Results after 6 weeks with our first client:
- 487 leads researched
- 185 outreach sequences
- 14.2% response rate
- 12 meetings booked
- $340K pipeline generated

The client's SDR generated $110K in the same period.

6/ What's next:
- Adding LinkedIn as a channel
- Building a self-improving scoring model
- Launching our Mission Control dashboard

If you're a funded startup struggling with outbound, DM me.

We're taking on 3 more clients this quarter.

posts.title: Post: Meridian Labs case study results
posts.body:
Our AI agent booked 12 meetings in 6 weeks for @MeridianLabs.

$340K in pipeline generated.
14.2% response rate (industry avg is 3-5%).

Their SDR generated $110K in the same period.

The kicker? Our agent runs 24/7 and never calls in sick.

Full case study dropping next week.

posts.title: Thread: From 0 to $18K MRR in 8 weeks with AI agents
posts.body:
8 weeks ago we had $0 in revenue.

Today we're at $18K MRR with 3 clients.

Here's the full transparent breakdown of how we got here building AI sales agents:

1/ Week 1-2: Built the MVP
No fancy pitch decks. We built a working prototype in 2 weeks.
- Lead scraping from X
- Basic enrichment
- Templated outreach

It was rough but it worked.

2/ Week 3-4: First pilot client (Meridian Labs)
Got our first client through X content. $3K/month pilot.
- Customized the agent for their ICP
- Started processing leads
- First meeting booked by Day 5

3/ Week 5-6: Results started compounding
Meridian pilot was crushing it:
- 12 meetings booked
- $340K pipeline
- 3x their SDR performance

Started posting results publicly. Inbound went crazy.

4/ Week 7-8: Scaled to 3 clients
- Meridian converted to $5K/month retainer
- Two new clients onboarded at $4K and $5K/month
- $18K MRR total

Our costs: ~$6K/month (APIs, infrastructure, data)

5/ What we learned:
- X is the best channel for AI/tech outreach (19.6% response rate)
- Building in public is the highest-ROI marketing strategy
- AI agents don't replace sales -- they handle the parts humans hate

More updates next week. DM me if you want to learn more.

posts.title: LinkedIn post: The future of B2B sales is hybrid
posts.body:
Hot take: The future of B2B sales isn't "AI replacing salespeople."

It's AI handling the 80% of work that salespeople hate:
- Lead research (hours of Googling)
- Writing personalized first touches
- Follow-up cadences
- Data entry and CRM updates

So salespeople can focus on the 20% they're great at:
- Building relationships
- Running discovery calls
- Closing deals
- Strategic account planning

At Black Sand, our AI agents handle the top of funnel entirely. But when a lead responds with interest, a human takes over.

This hybrid model gives us:
- 14% response rates (3-5x industry avg)
- 100% personalized outreach at scale
- Zero burnout on repetitive tasks

The companies that figure out this human+AI balance first will win the next decade of B2B sales.

What's your take -- where should the handoff between AI and human happen in the sales process?

posts.title: Thread: Our Mission Control dashboard (building in public)
posts.body:
We're building our own internal Mission Control dashboard.

One screen to see everything:
- All AI agents and their status
- Sales pipeline and lead stages
- Content performance metrics
- Team tasks and project progress
- Real-time financials

Here's what we're building and why (thread):

1/ The Problem
We were drowning in tabs. Notion for tasks, spreadsheets for pipeline, X analytics for content, Stripe for revenue.

Every morning started with 30 min of context-switching just to understand where things stood.

2/ The Solution
A single dark-themed dashboard that pulls from all our data sources.

Think of it as the cockpit for an AI-powered agency.

3/ Key Features
- Agent Status Panel: see all AI agents running in real-time
- Kanban Board: tasks across all projects
- Lead Pipeline: visual funnel from discovery to closed
- Content Calendar: upcoming posts + performance
- Finance Tracker: MRR, expenses, runway

4/ Tech Stack
- Next.js + TypeScript
- Tailwind CSS (dark mode everything)
- Supabase for real-time data
- Recharts for visualizations
- Deployed on Vercel

5/ Current status: 38% complete. Targeting MVP by March 14.

Will be open-sourcing the template once it's stable.

Drop a follow if you want to see the build progress.

posts.title: Blog: The Complete Guide to AI-Powered Sales Outreach in 2026
posts.body:
A comprehensive guide covering how AI agents are transforming B2B sales outreach. Covers lead discovery, research enrichment, personalized messaging at scale, multi-channel sequencing, and response handling. Includes real data from our client engagements and practical advice for founders considering AI sales tools.

Sections:
1. Why Traditional Outbound Is Broken
2. The AI Agent Approach to Sales
3. Building Your Lead Research Pipeline
4. Personalization That Actually Works
5. Multi-Channel Sequencing Strategy
6. Measuring What Matters
7. Case Study: How Meridian Labs 3x'd Their Pipeline
8. Getting Started: DIY vs. Agency
